results.description: DESCRIPTION:THE GOAL OF THE PROJECT IS TO DEVELOP A COST EFFECTIVE, ENVIRONMENTALLY FRIENDLY FOOD PACKAGING SYSTEM WHICH WILL REDUCE ENVIRONMENTAL WASTE.ACTIVITIES:THE PROJECT ACTIVITIES INCLUDE (1) DEVELOPING PACKAGING FILM WITH SIMILAR OR HIGHER  OXYGEN AND WATER VAPOR BARRIER PERFORMANCE TO MEET FOOD PROTECTION REQUIREMENTS; (2) EVALUATING FILM PRODUCTION PROCESSES THAT MAY FIT INTO EXISTING INFRASTRUCTURE; (3) ASSESSING CHEMICAL SAFETY OF PACKAGING FILM; AND (4) EVALUATING COST EFFICIENCY OF FILM.SUBRECIPIENT:NO SUBAWARDS ARE INCLUDED IN THIS ASSISTANCE AGREEMENT.OUTCOMES:DELIVERABLES FOR THIS PROJECT INCLUDE ANNUAL AND FINAL REPORTS. ADDITIONALLY, THE PROJECT IS EXPECTED TO PRODUCE JOURNAL MANUSCRIPTS DETAILING CHARACTERIZATION RESULTS OF FILM NANOCOATING AND PARAMETER OPTIMIZATION, AND A WEBSITE WHICH WILL EDUCATE THE PUBLIC ON HOW CURRENT PACKAGING FILM TECHNOLOGY AFFECTS OUR ENVIRONMENT AND PROMOTE THE IMPORTANCE OF ALTERNATIVE SOLUTIONS. THE EXPECTED OUTCOME OF THIS PROJECT WILL BE THE DEVELOPMENT OF A NEW BIODEGRADABLE PACKAGING FILM THAT AIMS TO ALTER CURRENT PLASTIC PACKAGING AND WASTE MANAGEMENT CONVENTIONS. DIRECT BENEFICIARIES INCLUDE PLASTIC MANUFACTURERS, PACKAGING INDUSTRIES, STATE AND LOCAL GOVERNMENTS, THE ENVIRONMENT, HUMAN HEALTH, AND THE GENERAL PUBLIC.
